steps.title: Spot the selective borrowing pattern
steps.content: The crucial clue is “all those words derive from the Zoque branch”—indicating the borrowed words come exclusively from one branch of the Mixe-Zoquean family, not from both.

steps.title: Apply the pattern to the timeline
steps.content: If the Mixe-Zoquean family were still unified during the Olmec period, borrowings would come from throughout the language, not exclusively from the Zoque branch. The fact that all borrowed words derive solely from Zoque indicates the family had already diverged into its two branches before the borrowing occurred—meaning Zoque was already distinct when Olmecs dominated. The other choices either propose broader borrowing not mentioned in the passage, reverse the timeline, or misidentify the family relationships. That matches choice C — C is correct.
